2606.10911.title: Ethical and Technical Limits of Deepfake Speech Datasets
2606.10911.paper_summary: Claims about the robustness and fairness of deepfake speech detectors are only as credible as the datasets used to train and evaluate those systems. We present a dataset-level audit of the deepfake speech landscape. We compile and analyze 39 deepfake speech datasets, examining key attributes including accessibility, documentation, demographic and language coverage, dataset scale, and the underlying bona fide speech sources. Our audit reveals two important takeaways. Firstly, fairness assessment is largely infeasible because most datasets lack demographic metadata, and only a few contain gender or language labels. This prevents any meaningful subgroup analysis and leaves other demographic attributes unaddressed. Secondly, we identify substantial overlap in underlying bona fide source corpora across datasets, which can undermine cross-dataset evaluation and lead to overstated generalization claims.
2606.10911.paper_summary_zh: 關於深偽語音檢測器的穩健性和公平性的聲明，其可信度僅與用於訓練和評估這些系統的數據集相關。我們對深偽語音的全景進行了數據集級別的審計。我們彙編並分析了39個深偽語音數據集，檢查了包括可訪問性、文檔、人口統計和語言覆蓋、數據集規模以及基礎真實語音來源在內的關鍵屬性。我們的審計揭示了兩個重要的結論。首先，由於大多數數據集缺乏人口統計元數據，且只有少數包含性別或語言標籤，因此公平性評估在很大程度上是不可行的。這阻礙了任何有意義的子群分析，並使其他人口統計屬性未得到解決。其次，我們發現不同數據集之間的基礎真實來源語料存在相當大的重疊，這可能會削弱跨數據集的評估並導致過度誇大的泛化聲明。
2606.10911.author: Vojtěch Staněk et.al.
2606.10911.authors: Vojtěch Staněk, Eva Trnovská, Kamil Malinka, Anton Firc
